disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No stridor (noisy/whistling breathing), able to speak in full sentences and swallow water; normal oxygenation
trigger_question_examples: Is your breathing noisy or whistling when you breathe in (stridor)?
Can you speak in full sentences and drink a sip of water?
Are your lips a normal colour?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No whistling, I can speak in full sentences and drink water fine, and my lips are normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No sudden voice change/hoarseness, no rapidly enlarging or acutely painful neck swelling
trigger_question_examples: Has your voice suddenly become very hoarse?
Is the neck lump suddenly bigger, hot, or very painful?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My voice is normal and the lump isn't suddenly bigger or painful.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sensation began with neck-focused hypervigilance and hyperventilation; goitre is longstanding and stable
trigger_question_examples: When did the tightness start relative to worrying/pressing on your neck?
Is the goitre something you've had stably for a while?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started as I worried and kept pressing on my neck; the lump has been there stably for a long time.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is my airway closing? Should I call an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Reassure based on the objective airway signs the patient can demonstrate (normal voice, full sentences, swallowing water, no stridor), explain throat-tightness is muscle tension from anxiety over a stable goitre, coach breathing and to stop palpating, and give a clear stridor/voice-change safety-net — without dispatching an ambulance for a patent airway.
